__schema0.title: Reference Object
__schema0.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

examples.description: A user is a type of person.
A pet is a type of animal.
A pet is a type of animal.

__schema1.title: Reference
__schema1.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

__schema5.title: summary
__schema5.description: A short summary which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema6.title: description
__schema6.description: A description which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema9.title: name
__schema9.description: The name of the query parameter. Case-sensitive.

__schema11.title: description
__schema11.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema16.description: Allows empty values (?param=). Deprecated in favor of schema-level validation.
__schema16.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema19.title: explode
__schema19.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema20.description: When true, allows reserved characters without percent-encoding.
__schema20.title: allowReserved

__schema23.title: Schema Object
__schema23.description: A Schema Object defines the shape of a JSON value.

__schema24.title: String Object
__schema24.description: A String Object defines the shape of a string value.

__schema25.title: title
__schema25.description: A title for the schema, used for documentation.

__schema26.title: description
__schema26.description: A description of the schema. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema27.title: default
__schema27.description: The default value for this schema.

__schema28.title: example
__schema28.description: An example value for this schema.

__schema29.title: enum
__schema29.description: An array of valid values for this schema.

__schema32.title: Discriminator
__schema32.description: When request bodies or response payloads may be one of a number of different schemas, a discriminator object can be used to aid in serialization, deserialization, and validation.

__schema33.title: propertyName
__schema33.description: The name of the property in the payload that will hold the discriminator value.

__schema34.title: mapping
__schema34.description: An object to hold mappings between payload values and schema names or references.

__schema39.title: XML
__schema39.description: A metadata object that allows for more fine-tuned XML model definitions.

__schema40.title: name
__schema40.description: Replaces the name of the element/attribute used for the described schema property.

__schema41.title: namespace
__schema41.description: The URI of the namespace definition.

__schema42.title: prefix
__schema42.description: The prefix to be used for the name.

__schema43.description: Declares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element.
__schema43.title: attribute

__schema45.description: May be used only for an array definition. Signifies whether the array is wrapped or not.
__schema45.title: wrapped

__schema49.title: ExternalDocumentation
__schema49.description: Allows referencing an external resource for extended documentation.

__schema50.title: description
__schema50.description: A short description of the target documentation.

__schema51.title: url
__schema51.description: The URL for the target documentation.

__schema53.title: readOnly
__schema53.description: When true, the property is only returned in responses, not accepted in requests.

__schema54.title: writeOnly
__schema54.description: When true, the property is only accepted in requests, not returned in responses.

__schema55.title: deprecated
__schema55.description: When true, indicates this schema is deprecated and should be avoided.

__schema56.title: nullable
__schema56.description: OpenAPI 3.0: Set to true to allow null values.

__schema58.title: format
__schema58.description: Semantic format hint for string values. Common formats: date-time, email, uuid, uri, hostname, ipv4, byte, binary.

__schema59.title: Pattern
__schema59.description: A regular expression pattern the string must match.

__schema65.title: Number Object
__schema65.description: A Number Object defines the shape of a number value.

__schema67.title: format
__schema67.description: Number precision format. Use 'float' for 32-bit single-precision or 'double' (default) for 64-bit double-precision IEEE 754.

__schema68.title: multipleOf
__schema68.description: Require the number to be a multiple of this value.

__schema70.title: minimum
__schema70.description: Inclusive lower bound for the number.

__schema72.title: maximum
__schema72.description: Inclusive upper bound for the number.

__schema74.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema74.description: When true, the minimum value is exclusive (value must be > minimum).

__schema76.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema76.description: When true, the maximum value is exclusive (value must be < maximum).

__schema78.title: Integer Object
__schema78.description: An Integer Object defines the shape of an integer value.

__schema80.title: format
__schema80.description: Integer size format. Use 'int32' for signed 32-bit (-2B to 2B) or 'int64' for signed 64-bit integers.

__schema81.title: multipleOf
__schema81.description: Require the integer to be a multiple of this value.

__schema83.title: minimum
__schema83.description: Inclusive lower bound for the integer.

__schema85.title: maximum
__schema85.description: Inclusive upper bound for the integer.

__schema87.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema87.description: When true, the minimum value is exclusive (value must be > minimum).

__schema89.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema89.description: When true, the maximum value is exclusive (value must be < maximum).

__schema91.title: Boolean Object
__schema91.description: A Boolean Object defines the shape of a boolean value.

__schema93.title: Array Object
__schema93.description: An Array Object defines the shape of an array value.

__schema103.title: Object Object
__schema103.description: An Object Object defines the shape of an object value.

__schema115.title: Composition Schema
__schema115.description: A schema using composition keywords (allOf, anyOf, oneOf, not) without a type constraint.

__schema116.title: allOf
__schema116.description: Require the instance to validate against all subschemas.

__schema118.title: anyOf
__schema118.description: Require the instance to validate against at least one subschema.

__schema120.title: oneOf
__schema120.description: Require the instance to validate against exactly one subschema.

__schema122.title: not
__schema122.description: Require the instance to NOT validate against the given subschema.

__schema125.title: examples
__schema125.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema132.title: externalValue
__schema132.description: A URL that points to the literal example.

__schema134.title: content
__schema134.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema136.title: MediaType
__schema136.description: Each Media Type Object provides schema and examples for the media type identified by its key.

__schema144.title: Encoding
__schema144.description: A single encoding definition applied to a single schema property.

__schema145.title: contentType
__schema145.description: The Content-Type for encoding a specific property.

__schema148.title: Header
__schema148.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema149.title: Header
__schema149.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema150.title: description
__schema150.description: A brief description of the parameter.

__schema151.description: Determines whether this parameter is mandatory.
__schema151.title: required

__schema153.description: Specifies that a parameter is deprecated.
__schema153.title: deprecated

__schema155.description: Sets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters.
__schema155.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema157.title: style
__schema157.description: Describes how the parameter value will be serialized.

__schema184.title: name
__schema184.description: The name of the path parameter. Must match a template expression in the path.

__schema186.title: description
__schema186.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema187.description: Path parameters must always be required.
__schema187.title: required

__schema191.title: explode
__schema191.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema194.title: examples
__schema194.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema196.title: content
__schema196.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema200.title: name
__schema200.description: The name of the header. Case-insensitive.

__schema202.title: description
__schema202.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema207.title: style
__schema207.description: Serialization style. Only 'simple' is valid for headers.

__schema208.title: explode
__schema208.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema211.title: examples
__schema211.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema213.title: content
__schema213.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema219.title: description
__schema219.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema224.title: style
__schema224.description: Serialization style. Only 'form' is valid for cookies.

__schema225.title: explode
__schema225.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema228.title: examples
__schema228.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema230.title: content
__schema230.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.
